majorArcana.name: The Fool
majorArcana.title: Cosmic Ecstasy
majorArcana.narrative: The Fool is the Soul, the Spirit, beginning its journey. The Fool leaps into evolution, into Life, in order to experience everything. He is each individual that feels separated from Unity — carrying everything needed inside his bag, hidden for him to remember and find out. The Sun, the Light, is always behind him for guidance and support. He represents anyone who seeks knowledge of who we are, where we came from, and where we are going.
majorArcana.upright: The start of a trip, journey, adventure, experience or project; a state of no-experience; innocence; joker.
majorArcana.reversed: Indecisiveness; fear of a new beginning, of taking the leap to the unknown.
symbols: The bag on the stick (hidden gifts and talents)
The white rose (purity)
The small dog (worldly instincts)
The sun behind him (divine guidance)
The flowery circles on his robe (cycles of nature and spirit)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the meaning of the flowery circles on the Fool's robe?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: Where in your life are you being called to leap into the unknown with trust and innocence?

majorArcana.name: The Magician
majorArcana.title: Cosmic Knowledge
majorArcana.narrative: The Magician is the Fool on his 1st step of evolution and growth — the Soul starting to realize the tools it is carrying and how to use them. The four Alchemical Elements rest on his table. He uses the Hermetic Law of 'As Above, So Below' to download wisdom from Heavens to Earth, learning the art of Manifestation and the Law of Attraction.
majorArcana.upright: Conscious will power, change, working with the Elements, invention, creativity, magical powers, manifestation.
majorArcana.reversed: Lack of will, the need to get inventive or creative to get things done.
symbols: The infinity symbol above his head (endless potential)
The four tools on the table (the four elements/suits)
One hand pointing up, one down (As Above So Below)
The red roses and white lilies (desire and purity)
The wand raised to heaven (channeling divine will)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the meaning of the infinity symbol above his head?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What tools and resources do you already have that you are not fully using?

majorArcana.name: The High Priestess
majorArcana.title: Cosmic Presence
majorArcana.narrative: The High Priestess is the Fool understanding the subconscious and how to work with it. She holds and guards everything hidden, occult, mysterious and unknown — the unwritten Laws of Life and the Universe. She sits between the two pillars of Solomon's Temple, portraying Duality and Balance, ever-virgin and never-changing like Water. She is the 1 True TRUTH, uniting all religions and dogmas.
majorArcana.upright: Sub-consciousness, mystery, hidden knowledge, inner wisdom, depth, challenging answers and results.
majorArcana.reversed: The knowledge will appear, all will be clear, things will reveal themselves to you soon.
symbols: The two pillars B and J (duality — Boaz and Jachin)
The veil with pomegranates (the boundary between worlds)
The scroll in her lap (hidden law)
The crescent moon at her feet (cycles and intuition)
The cross on her chest (balance of elements)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is hidden behind the veil of the High Priestess?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What is your inner voice trying to tell you that you have not yet been willing to hear?

majorArcana.name: The Empress
majorArcana.title: Cosmic Love
majorArcana.narrative: The Empress is the Fool learning how to be creative and create. She represents the Mother of Creation, Mother Earth, the Goddess of Fertility. The result of the Magician and the High Priestess, she is the Universal-Womb to all phenomenon, the gate of Heaven. Everywhere she goes — flowers bloom. She can destroy and create, grow and renew.
majorArcana.upright: Creation, fertility, the birth of something with good results, cultivating the fruits.
majorArcana.reversed: Infertility, there is a need to be more creative.
symbols: The 12 stars on her crown (12 signs of the zodiac)
The wheat at her feet (earthly abundance)
The water stream (creative imagination)
Her cushioned throne in nature (comfort in the material world)
The Venus symbol on her heart shield (the power of love)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What represents the 12 stars on the crown of the Empress?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: Where in your life are you being invited to nurture, create, or allow something to grow naturally?

majorArcana.name: The Emperor
majorArcana.title: Cosmic Strength
majorArcana.narrative: The Emperor is the Fool learning what Knowledge is. He is the Father of Creation, sitting stable on his throne, creating through logic, intellect, and knowledge. His path bridges the 'Perfect Father' archetype and our Higher Self, helping us build our Inner-Light from Darkness. He is rigid, precise, and rationalizes all things — older and more ancient than Time itself.
majorArcana.upright: Knowledge, information, understanding, laws of creation, stability, masculine energy, willpower, ruling.
majorArcana.reversed: Lack of knowledge, lack of information or understanding, wrong use of the laws of creation.
symbols: The orb and cross he holds (dominion over the material world)
The ram heads on his throne (Aries energy — force and leadership)
The barren mountains behind him (the stark reality of pure structure)
His armor under the robe (readiness for any challenge)
The ankh in his hand (life force)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the meaning of the cross and orb that the Emperor holds?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: Where do you need to bring more structure, discipline, or clarity to build something lasting?

majorArcana.name: The Hierophant
majorArcana.title: Power to Initiate
majorArcana.narrative: The Hierophant is the Fool realizing his own personal Spiritual-Guide. He represents a Spiritual Teacher, the teacher of the mysteries, holding the keys to Heaven and Hell — a metaphor for our personal Guardian Angel. His path connects the Micro to the Macro, like a hook. When he speaks, we must be quiet and listen — this is our initiation to the Universal teacher.
majorArcana.upright: Spiritual guide, inner advisor, authority, religion, tradition, silence to hear answers.
majorArcana.reversed: Advising in an unorthodox way, the need to stop and listen.
symbols: The two crossed keys (keys to heaven and earth)
The three-tiered crown (mastery over three worlds)
The two monks kneeling (seekers receiving wisdom)
The triple cross staff (spiritual authority)
The pillars (duality held in balance by wisdom)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the meaning of the 2 crossed keys?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What spiritual teaching, tradition, or inner guidance are you being asked to honor and listen to right now?

majorArcana.name: The Lovers
majorArcana.title: The Chemical Wedding
majorArcana.narrative: The Lovers is the Fool realizing the importance and holiness of his Spiritual journey. This card is the harmony between consciousness and sub-consciousness, masculine and feminine, mind and heart, yin and yang — the card of understanding and overcoming Duality and Polarity. The Lovers represent a 'chemical wedding' of ourselves with ourselves, opening the collective sub-consciousness to receive Divine wisdom.
majorArcana.upright: Love, harmony inside and outside, polarity, 2 hemispheres of the brain, falling in love with your Self.
majorArcana.reversed: Lack of Self-love, lack of harmony.
symbols: The two trees (Tree of Knowledge and Tree of Life)
The angel above (divine blessing on the union)
The man looking at the woman, woman looking at the angel (earth — heaven connection)
The sun (consciousness and illumination)
The snake in the tree (temptation and wisdom)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the meaning of the 2 Trees?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: Where in your life are you being called to choose love — especially self-love — over fear or division?

majorArcana.name: The Chariot
majorArcana.title: Giving All for Wisdom
majorArcana.narrative: The Chariot is the Fool understanding his personal responsibility over his destiny and life. The chariot is our consciousness which moves inside evolution — control through balance and harmony. The fuel for its movement is the balance between the two sphinxes: black and white, mind and heart, activity and passivity. Only when open to Divine energy does the velocity increase.
majorArcana.upright: Movement, journey, travel, responsibility and respect for Life, ambition, ability to control our Evolution, using Duality for movement.
majorArcana.reversed: Inability to navigate Life, navigating toward a wrong direction, not using Duality properly.
symbols: The two sphinxes (duality as the fuel for movement)
The canopy of stars (cosmic guidance)
The square on his chest (earth mastery)
The city behind him (the world left behind)
The charioteer with no reins (control through will, not force)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What does the charioteer control the sphinxes with if he holds no reins?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What area of your life needs you to take the reins and move forward with focused intention?

majorArcana.name: Strength
majorArcana.title: Learning to use Power
majorArcana.yetziраticName: The Intelligence of all the activities and spiritual beings
majorArcana.narrative: Strength is the Fool remembering what he had. This card speaks of a Spiritual-Force — a higher-energy — pointing to a person who has reached Divine-protection from Above, allowing the seemingly impossible. The enlightened person controls animalistic potential through will power — the wise man who controls the savage beast in a humanitarian way. This is the awakening of the Kundalini Energy.
majorArcana.upright: Spiritual power, supernatural abilities, knowledge of strength, connection to higher dimensions, high-energies.
majorArcana.reversed: Lack of knowledge about supernatural abilities, inability to connect to higher dimensions and high-energies.
symbols: The infinity symbol (same as the Magician — mastery over infinite potential)
The white robe (spiritual purity)
The lion (primal force and passion)
The garland of flowers (nature and gentleness)
The woman's calm posture (strength through love, not force)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the meaning of the infinity sign above the character's head? How does it relate to the Magician?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: Where in your life does true strength require gentleness, patience, or compassion rather than force?

majorArcana.name: The Hermit
majorArcana.title: Absorbing Power
majorArcana.narrative: The Hermit is the Fool with a strong and solid Inner-Light, able to share it with other Truth-Seekers. He symbolizes a spiritual-leader who does not seek students — static on the mountaintop — but always appears when needed. He is the picture of 'When the student is ready, the teacher appears.' All of his knowledge and wisdom is useless if not shared with others.
majorArcana.upright: Spiritual leader, the ability to give good advice.
majorArcana.reversed: The need for a spiritual teacher, the need for guidance, looking for advice.
symbols: The lantern with the six-pointed star (the Star of David — wisdom lighting the path)
The staff (authority and support on the journey)
The mountaintop (pinnacle of spiritual attainment)
The grey cloak (invisibility — wisdom does not seek attention)
The six-pointed star (inner light)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: How many tips can we see in the shining star?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What wisdom have you gained through solitude or hardship that is now ready to be shared?

majorArcana.name: Wheel of Fortune
majorArcana.title: Undoing Past Experience
majorArcana.narrative: The Wheel of Fortune is the Fool understanding the Law of Cause and Effect, the Law of Karma, and the turning Cycles in Life. The wheel connects individuality with the Higher-self. This is NOT a wheel of accidents or coincidences — there are none in the Universe. Its purpose is to close cycles and complete activities. 10 is the beginning of a new cycle in an upward spiral.
majorArcana.upright: Success with the 'flow' of Life, knowing of and understanding Cause and Effect, balance, progression.
majorArcana.reversed: Moving backwards, stopping, inertia, not understanding the Laws of the Universe.
symbols: The four fixed signs of the zodiac in the corners (bull, lion, eagle, man — the constants)
The snake descending (life-force descending into creation)
Anubis ascending (evolution of thought through intuition)
The Sphinx at the top (balance and stability in eternal change)
The Hebrew letters YHVH on the wheel (divine names)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: Why does the Sphinx sit atop the Wheel and never fall, no matter how fast it spins?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What cycle in your life is completing? What new cycle is beginning?

majorArcana.name: Justice
majorArcana.title: Balancing Forces
majorArcana.narrative: Justice is the Fool stopping to judge itself and others. It is a state of Mind — learning to be honest, fair, objective and non-judgmental. The enforcer of the Law of Karma, it is the Cause for the Effect. We can decide consciously to bring balance to our life, or this decision will be made for us naturally, sometimes painfully. The justice card represents 'You reap what you sow.'
majorArcana.upright: Justice in Life, do not judge others, speak the Truth, good sign for legal issues.
majorArcana.reversed: Difficulty with legal issues, the need to be a better person doing positive actions, liar.
symbols: The scales (perfect balance of opposing forces)
The sword pointing upward (discernment cuts through illusion)
The veil behind her (hidden truth about to be revealed)
The square on her crown (logical, ordered thinking)
The two pillars (duality held in balance)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is behind the veil?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: Where in your life are you being called to act with greater honesty, fairness, or accountability?

majorArcana.name: The Hanged Man
majorArcana.title: Radiating Love
majorArcana.narrative: The Hanged Man is the Fool changing its perspective over Life and Death. Things are not what they seem — we must look beneath the surface. This is a reversal of perspective, like drowning in the current of the ONE consciousness. He hangs in astonishing peace and tranquility, centered on himself but aware of the 'One Force' everywhere. This is the crucifixion of the Ego toward selfless help to others.
majorArcana.upright: Change of perspective, new perception, changing direction, attention to dreams.
majorArcana.reversed: Resistance to necessary change, clinging to old perspectives, refusing to surrender.
symbols: The halo of light around his head (enlightenment through surrender)
The T-shaped tree (the Tau cross — initiation)
The relaxed face (peace in suspension)
One leg crossed behind the other (the number 4 — earth mastery)
The inverted position (complete reversal of normal awareness)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: Why does the Hanged Man appear at peace rather than in distress?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What situation in your life is asking you to stop struggling and simply surrender to a new perspective?

majorArcana.name: Death
majorArcana.title: Transcendence
majorArcana.narrative: Death is the Fool's biggest change. The death card is the deepest change in our life — the twin brother of Life. This is the process called 'The Dark Night of the Soul,' forcing us to forgo desires for a union with our inner-light. The death is a new psychological attunement, a transformation where individuality relinquishes everything to walk the darkness of night with faith in resurrection. Death is but the Child of the Great Transformers.
majorArcana.upright: A huge change, something died inside, transformation, positive change.
majorArcana.reversed: The need for a huge change, feeling stuck, tendency to get stuck.
symbols: The skeleton in armor (death spares no one regardless of status)
The white rose (purity and renewal)
The two towers (the gateway to the beyond)
The setting sun (death and rebirth of each day)
The king lying fallen (even rulers must submit to transformation)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the meaning of the 2 towers in the background?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What in your life has already died or is ready to die — and what is waiting to be reborn?

majorArcana.name: Temperance
majorArcana.title: Rising on the Planes
majorArcana.narrative: Temperance is the Fool realizing that he is an Angel. This card is the ability to keep composure, restraint, and calmness — the conscious ability to be present in two worlds simultaneously. Temperance is the point where all questions are answered. The path creates something new, greater than the sum of its parts — a continuous process of testing and measuring, preparing the personality and body to receive more Light.
majorArcana.upright: The ability to stay composed in any situation.
majorArcana.reversed: The need to learn how to remain composed.
symbols: The two cups exchanging liquid (the flow of energy between worlds)
One foot on land, one in water (balance between realms)
The triangle within a square on the robe (spirit within matter)
The path leading to the sun (the spiritual path ahead)
The angel's wings (higher nature)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the deeper meaning of the liquid flowing between the two cups?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: Where in your life are you being asked to find balance, patience, or flow rather than forcing an outcome?

majorArcana.name: The Devil
majorArcana.title: Talismanic Work
majorArcana.narrative: The Devil is the moment when the Fool is tested, mocked, or provoked. He is both the tempter and the redeemer — not an evil figure separate from us, but the archetype of fear and darkness. The card is meant to be humorous, for our belief in the illusion of matter is laughable. Knowledge alone is enough to turn the Devil into something new. Laughter and a healthy sense of humor are among the tools for transcending him.
majorArcana.upright: Negative use of qualities, harmful intentions, addictions, unhealthy surrender.
majorArcana.reversed: Beginning of freedom, release from addictions, the need to recognize the light within us in order to face our demons.
symbols: The loose chains around the figures' necks (bondage that can be released at any time)
The inverted pentagram (material world over spirit)
The torch pointing downward (misuse of fire/will)
The half-human, half-animal figure (the animal nature within us)
The two chained figures (the Lovers in shadow)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: Why do the characters stay chained when the chains around their necks are loose enough to remove?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What fear, addiction, or illusion is keeping you chained — and what would it feel like to simply remove that chain?

majorArcana.name: The Tower
majorArcana.title: Silencing Thought
majorArcana.narrative: The Tower is the Fool rising above fears and illusions. This card is the destruction of everything founded on false foundations — the transcendence of all that makes us afraid. The Tower is the 'I.' The lightning strike is the sudden realization of our true identity. What we receive is always what we need, but not necessarily what we want. Another name for this card is 'War' — inner spiritual growth must be both painful and awe-inspiring.
majorArcana.upright: Destruction, chaos, difficulties arising for the sake of transcendence.
majorArcana.reversed: Negative destruction without positive results.
symbols: The falling crown (the ego dethroned by truth)
The lightning bolt from the heavens (sudden divine illumination)
The figures falling (the old self being dismantled)
The 22 flames (the 22 paths of the Tree of Life)
The tower on the mountaintop (the ego built high and proud)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the meaning of the falling crown?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What false foundation, belief, or structure in your life needs to fall so something truer can be built?

majorArcana.name: The Star
majorArcana.title: Expanding Consciousness
majorArcana.narrative: The Star is the Fool after the trials, with expanded awareness. Connected to Temperance but more advanced, it is a bridge between the visible and invisible worlds. When we succeed in truly meditating, we understand it is not we who do meditation — meditation does us. The great star symbolizes the merging and convergence of many lights shining from one high point. The naked figure symbolizes the absence of secrets.
majorArcana.upright: Spiritual support, intuition, faith, guidance, spiritual bridge.
majorArcana.reversed: Lack of spiritual support, lack of intuition, lack of faith.
symbols: The large eight-pointed star (the Star of Venus — beauty and hope)
The seven smaller stars (the seven chakras or seven planets)
The naked woman (transparency — no secrets)
The two vessels pouring water (separation and reunion of emotions)
The bird in the tree (the ibis of Thoth — wisdom)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the meaning of the big Star?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: After recent challenges, what hope or renewed faith is beginning to emerge within you?

majorArcana.name: The Moon
majorArcana.title: Past Lives, Archetypes
majorArcana.narrative: The Moon is the Fool seeing the unseen and the hidden. It represents the open collective subconscious and the presence of supernatural and extrasensory forces. The Moon organizes our body and consciousness during sleep — the awareness that continues to function when the rest of the mind is at rest. When we conquer our dreams and slay our dragons, we gain the ability to use these qualities within ourselves.
majorArcana.upright: Mystery revealed, supernatural and extrasensory abilities, lucid dreams, open collective subconscious.
majorArcana.reversed: Mystery hidden, need to develop supernatural abilities, closed collective subconscious.
symbols: The crayfish emerging from the water (the organic evolution of humankind from the deep)
The two towers (the threshold between the known and unknown)
The dog and wolf howling (the domesticated and wild within us)
The path leading to the mountains (the long unknown journey)
The reflection of the moon in the water (illusion versus reality)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the difference between the dog and the wolf howling at the Moon?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What dream, vision, or subconscious feeling is trying to reach your conscious awareness right now?

majorArcana.name: The Sun
majorArcana.title: Invoking Light of Self
majorArcana.narrative: The Sun is the Fool radiating light into the world. The soul that has passed the initiation of the Sun can do exactly what the Sun does — give light to the world. Now we know who we are, where we came from, and where we are going. We return to innocence, become young again, and renew ourselves. This is also the point where sexuality is understood as the secret power of evolution.
majorArcana.upright: Success, light, wisdom, understanding, knowledge, life.
majorArcana.reversed: The need to seek light, knowledge, and success within.
symbols: The naked child on the horse (innocence and mastery united)
The sunflowers (vitality turning always toward the light)
The red banner (victory and passion)
The stone wall (the boundary between the garden of growth and the outer world)
The sun with a face (the conscious, living intelligence of light)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: Why does the child and horse not look towards the Sun?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: Where in your life are you ready to step fully into your light and share your radiance with others?

majorArcana.name: Judgement
majorArcana.title: Death and Rebirth
majorArcana.narrative: Judgement is the Fool awakening — his final understanding. It speaks of resurrection, a rebirth after death — the merging of conscious, subconscious, and collective unconscious. This is the moment when the soul discovers the Key of Life and fully understands the Tree of Life. The Archangel arrives once the fire of passion and will has been ignited. Everything judged as unworthy or ineffective is slowly burned in the fire.
majorArcana.upright: Rebirth, new life, resurrection, something ending, a moment to celebrate with joy.
majorArcana.reversed: The need to continue the journey, to seek new things.
symbols: The Archangel Gabriel blowing the trumpet (the call to awakening)
The cross on the flag (balance of the elements)
The figures rising from coffins (resurrection of the self)
The mountains of ice (obstacles overcome)
The grey figures (the soul freed from the limitations of matter)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the meaning of the Cross on the Archangel's banner?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What calling or awakening have you been hearing from within — and are you ready to answer it?

majorArcana.name: The World
majorArcana.title: Exploring the Aether
majorArcana.narrative: The World is the Fool whose time has come to leave Earth, after many lifetimes. It is a gate — the doorway out for human beings, and the doorway in for angels. After complete mastery over ourselves and our environment, the One Power becomes the reality of our lives. Between Alpha and Omega, there is a whole world. Some refer to this path as 'Heaven' or 'Nirvana.'
majorArcana.upright: Happy ending, success, completion of the journey, closure of a phase or situation, beginning of new life.
majorArcana.reversed: Stagnation, no movement, no progress, negative outcomes.
symbols: The dancing figure (freedom, joy, and mastery)
The laurel wreath (victory and completion)
The scarf (the eternal spiral of existence)
The four fixed signs in the corners (mastery over all four elements)
The two wands (the same wands the Magician held — the journey is complete)
majorArcana.mysticalQuestion: What is the meaning of the Scarf?
majorArcana.dailyQuestion: What major cycle or chapter in your life is now complete — and what does that completion feel like?

pentacles.name: Ace of Pentacles
pentacles.title: The Roots of the Powers of Earth
pentacles.upright: Root of material matters, physical health, grounding major endeavors, real opportunities.
pentacles.reversed: Misuse of material resources, need to recognize new opportunities.
pentacles.narrative: The visible world and everything within it, the magical ability to connect heaven and earth, living in paradise.

pentacles.name: Two of Pentacles
pentacles.title: Harmonious Change
pentacles.upright: Parallel work, balancing two factors, awareness of rising and falling phases.
pentacles.reversed: Inability to balance two factors simultaneously, lack of harmonious action.
pentacles.narrative: Understanding that physical experiences are a mutable tool for spiritual development, balance, and calm during physical changes.

pentacles.name: Three of Pentacles
pentacles.title: Material Works
pentacles.upright: Harmony and agreement in business matters, good working relationships, professionalism.
pentacles.reversed: Disagreement, disharmony, difficulty cooperating with others.
pentacles.narrative: Work reflecting spiritual ideals, understanding between employee and employer, appreciation, and fair compensation.

pentacles.name: Four of Pentacles
pentacles.title: Earthly Power
pentacles.upright: Material ambition, success in work, material gain, excellent ability to work and succeed.
pentacles.narrative: Focus on material matters and the desire to maintain profits, self-stability enabling support for the less fortunate.

pentacles.name: Five of Pentacles
pentacles.title: Earthly Trouble
pentacles.upright: Poverty, illness, struggle or physical difficulty, hard times, unemployment.
pentacles.narrative: Spiritual growth arising from dealing with disabilities, illness, or lack of resources, karmic balance.

pentacles.name: Six of Pentacles
pentacles.title: Material Success
pentacles.upright: Sharing, giving, generosity, social work, helping, selflessness, altruism.
pentacles.narrative: Sharing with those in need, a higher drive to give to others in a balanced way fostering spiritual growth.

pentacles.name: Seven of Pentacles
pentacles.title: Success Unfulfilled
pentacles.upright: Dissatisfaction with our actions, patience, waiting for results, letting time take its course.
pentacles.narrative: The stage between accepting what exists and worrying about what will be, things beyond our control.

pentacles.name: Eight of Pentacles
pentacles.title: Prudence
pentacles.upright: Professionalism in actions, dedication to work, consistency and diligence, harmony in labor.
pentacles.reversed: Lack of perseverance, dissatisfaction, unhappiness in work.
pentacles.narrative: Training brings perfection, skill developed through persistence and self-discipline.

pentacles.name: Nine of Pentacles
pentacles.title: Material Gain
pentacles.upright: Material success, abundance, having everything needed, appreciation of results, gratitude.
pentacles.reversed: Risk of losing everything, seeing the glass as half empty, lack of gratitude.
pentacles.narrative: Financial and material security achieved, opportunity to stop focusing on possessions and begin a spiritual journey.

pentacles.name: Ten of Pentacles
pentacles.title: Wealth
pentacles.upright: Stable foundation, successful acquisitions, completed work, material abundance, good health.
pentacles.reversed: Material loss, weak, unstable, or false foundation.
pentacles.narrative: Achievement of all fruits and physical completion, family and home, wealth and prosperity, understanding the laws of creation.

swords.name: Ace of Swords
swords.title: The Roots of the Powers of Air
swords.upright: Root of thoughts, clarity of mind, source of intellect, precise formulation of ideas.
swords.narrative: A tool for shaping, organizing, and creating thoughts, analytical and scientific thinking, sharp divine intellect.

swords.name: Two of Swords
swords.title: Peace Restored
swords.narrative: Internal analysis before action, detachment from emotional and external distractions, balance and calm before taking steps.

swords.name: Three of Swords
swords.title: Sorrow
swords.upright: Confused thoughts affecting emotions, sinking into contemplation.
swords.reversed: Solution arrives, confusion clears, release from sorrow.
swords.narrative: Pain and suffering, relief through tears, desire to understand the source of suffering and negative thoughts, inner search.

swords.name: Four of Swords
swords.title: Rest from Strife
swords.upright: Rest from thought, renovation of ideas, need for pause, partial surrender.
swords.narrative: Temporary withdrawal from the world, release of fixed opinions, acceptance of new ideas, prayer.

swords.name: Five of Swords
swords.title: Defeat
swords.upright: Failure, defeat, internal conflict, ending of struggle due to exhaustion.
swords.narrative: Action and reaction, momentary power over others, knowing when and what to fight for and when to step back.

swords.name: Six of Swords
swords.title: Earned Success
swords.upright: Journey of consciousness, conceptual journey leading to success, transformation, desire for resolution.
swords.narrative: Carefully planned journey, faith in the unseen, good guidance toward a different path or higher awareness.

swords.name: Seven of Swords
swords.title: Unstable Effort
swords.upright: Escaping thoughts, distraction, confusion, adopting ideas from others, reconsideration.
swords.reversed: Running away with ideas that are not yours.
swords.narrative: Creating a plan requires leaving some ideas behind, reservations regarding the ideas abandoned.

swords.name: Eight of Swords
swords.title: Shortened Force
swords.upright: Mental imprisonment, trapped in thoughts, disturbances, obsessions, addictions, stagnation.
swords.narrative: Intention, mental resilience, deep insight leading to freedom despite obstacles in perception and action.

swords.name: Nine of Swords
swords.title: Despair and Cruelty
swords.upright: Obsessive mind causing issues, difficulty finding solutions, sleep problems.
swords.reversed: Good times approaching, realization that all is illusion, everything changes, improved sleep.
swords.narrative: Analysis of faulty thought patterns for mental healing, prayer for guidance, the pain of letting go of desires.

swords.name: Ten of Swords
swords.title: Ruin
swords.upright: Total darkness before sunrise, death of thought, destruction of intellect, awakening, rebirth.
swords.reversed: Courage to recover, determination to awaken, end of darkness.
swords.narrative: Base of the ladder and beginning of relief, understanding that everything changes, acceptance bringing the dawn of a new day.

cups.name: Ace of Cups
cups.title: The Roots of the Powers of Water
cups.upright: Pure intention, open heart, source of feeling, fellowship, giving, warmth.
cups.reversed: Emotional coldness, blocked emotions, lack of feeling.
cups.narrative: An open vessel containing emotions and imagination, pure fluid energy flowing through us and nourishing the world like water.

cups.name: Two of Cups
cups.title: Love
cups.upright: Union and harmony of opposites, giving, and the possibility to share the heart.
cups.reversed: Separation, division, relationship issues, emotional difficulties.
cups.narrative: Intimate love between inner or outer masculine and feminine, healing and renewal, higher divine wisdom.

cups.name: Three of Cups
cups.title: Abundance
cups.upright: Joy, reason to celebrate, birth, abundance, happiness.
cups.reversed: Celebration ends, conclusion of happiness, sadness.
cups.narrative: Celebration, happiness, understanding, agreement, hope, and gratitude, creative possibilities.

cups.name: Four of Cups
cups.title: Pleasure
cups.upright: Opportunity, gift from above, special life moment, new emotion, contemplation, calm.
cups.reversed: Missed opportunity, closed off to receiving a gift.
cups.narrative: Reflection, inner choice power, patience, intuition, waiting for the right moment and the right opportunity to come.

cups.name: Five of Cups
cups.title: Loss in Pleasure
cups.upright: Frustration, stagnation, guilt, loss, disappointment, and negative emotions about to transform.
cups.narrative: Need to mourn, stagnation regarding loss, depression, sadness, anger, need for life change.

cups.name: Six of Cups
cups.title: Joy
cups.upright: Balanced and stable emotions, joy, happiness, true love, uplifting encounter with the past.
cups.reversed: Connection to the past, need to resolve unfinished matters, confronting past issues.
cups.narrative: Childlike innocence, playfulness, fantasies, unconditional love, support from divine guidance.

cups.name: Seven of Cups
cups.title: Illusory Success
cups.upright: Emotional confusion, many possibilities, conflicting feelings, intense emotions.
cups.reversed: Beginning of emotional clarity after confusion.
cups.narrative: Developed creative imagination, grounding visions into reality, fulfilling dreams, making the right choice among many options.

cups.name: Eight of Cups
cups.title: Abandoned Success
cups.upright: Withdrawal for clarification, inward reflection to change emotional perspective.
cups.narrative: Pause from a sensitive situation, mental clearing, shift in perspective.

cups.name: Nine of Cups
cups.title: Material Happiness
cups.upright: Positive emotional life, satisfaction, contentment, fulfillment, success in all matters.
cups.narrative: Confidence in achieving desires, understanding that what comes to us depends on our inner world.

cups.name: Ten of Cups
cups.title: Perfected Success
cups.upright: Gratitude, joy, success, amazement, unity, family connection.
cups.narrative: Family celebration, good news, dreams coming true, coexistence and acceptance, healing family relationships.

wands.name: Ace of Wands
wands.title: The Roots of the Powers of Fire
wands.upright: The beginning of creation, the start of the spiritual journey, the root of morality.
wands.reversed: A false or superficial beginning, lack of values and spiritual principles.
wands.narrative: Divine inspiration, willpower, and vitality flowing from the unseen world. The importance of inner awareness.

wands.name: Two of Wands
wands.title: The Lord of Dominion
wands.upright: Direction, intention, inner or outer spiritual guidance, positive advice.
wands.narrative: Environmental overview, the intention to begin a project that brings inner focus, conquering the world.

wands.name: Three of Wands
wands.title: Established Strength
wands.upright: Progress along the spiritual journey, correct direction.
wands.reversed: Difficulty moving forward, lack of hope and faith.
wands.narrative: Ideas and inspiration, support and assistance from others, or walking the spiritual path alone with hope and faith.

wands.name: Four of Wands
wands.title: Perfected Work
wands.upright: A spiritual mission successfully completed with positive results, harmony, and joy.
wands.narrative: Spiritual celebration, companionship, honoring ancient traditions and rituals, creation of new traditions.

wands.name: Five of Wands
wands.title: Strife
wands.upright: Conflicts, problems, and spiritual challenges, changes arising from them.
wands.narrative: Diverse and contrasting opinions, divided attention, conflict of interests, group will overriding individual will.

wands.name: Six of Wands
wands.title: Victory
wands.upright: Triumph over conflict, harmony, spiritual progress — internal or external.
wands.narrative: Embodiment of a higher purpose, admiration from others as a hero, leader, or savior.

wands.name: Seven of Wands
wands.title: Valor
wands.upright: Bravery, defending ideals without fear or hesitation.
wands.narrative: Courage and inner confidence against opposition, standing your ground despite threats, spiritual obstacles, transcendence.

wands.name: Eight of Wands
wands.title: Swiftness
wands.upright: Experiences and thoughts that come and go quickly, spiritual ideas, good opportunities.
wands.reversed: Experiences and thoughts that linger for a long time, missed opportunities.
wands.narrative: Inspiration flying swiftly through open space, upward or downward.

wands.name: Nine of Wands
wands.title: Great Strength
wands.upright: Preserved spiritual energy, extrasensory abilities, guarding hidden knowledge.
wands.reversed: Spiritual obstacles that must be transcended and overcome.
wands.narrative: Alertness, attentiveness, listening, focus, readiness to respond wisely to whatever may come.

wands.name: Ten of Wands
wands.title: Oppression
wands.upright: Determination, decisiveness to take the final step, end of a journey.
wands.narrative: Strong willpower, focused intention, an important goal within reach, nearing completion of a task.

pentacles.name: Page of Pentacles
pentacles.title: The Princess of the Echoing Hills / The Rose of the Palace of Earth
pentacles.upright: Hesitation and confusion due to inexperience, pressure to succeed from focusing on material considerations, contentment with modest achievements.
pentacles.narrative: New student of material forces, awareness of surroundings, body, and physical health. Understanding that the material world is spirit enclosed in form — the body as a temple.
pentacles.reversed: Lack of focus on practical matters, daydreaming without grounding, avoiding responsibility, poor financial decisions due to inexperience.

pentacles.name: Knight of Pentacles
pentacles.title: The Prince of the Chariot of Earth
pentacles.upright: Pursuit of money and wealth, proper direction in integrating personal skills with career, vision not yet realized, progress slow and steady, readiness to use power wisely.
pentacles.narrative: Internalizing learning about the power of matter as a spiritual tool, dedicating oneself to handling daily experiences — work, finances, environment, and health — for personal growth.
pentacles.reversed: Stagnation in material matters, excessive focus on security at the expense of growth, stubbornness, unwillingness to change approach.

pentacles.name: Queen of Pentacles
pentacles.title: The Queen of the Thrones of Earth
pentacles.upright: Physical and material stability, good health, conservatism, control over environment, ability to remain fixed and stable.
pentacles.narrative: Willingness to care for others, ability to overcome obstacles, nurturing, hard work, clear intellect, resilience, sensuality, understanding spiritual reality as presented in material reality.
pentacles.reversed: Neglect of home or health, financial insecurity, smothering or over-controlling behavior, disconnection from the body and natural cycles.

pentacles.name: King of Pentacles
pentacles.title: The Lord of the Wide and Fertile Land / The King of the Spirits of Earth
pentacles.upright: Maturity and full control in the manifestation process, experience and expertise in the material dimension, broad vision, self-restraint, humility, sense of material security.
pentacles.narrative: Material prosperity, health, leadership, fertility, contentment, peace, understanding that power comes from the hidden world of the soul and accumulated experiences.
pentacles.reversed: Misuse of material power, corruption, greed, poor financial management, confusing wealth with worth, rigidity in the face of necessary change.

swords.name: Page of Swords
swords.title: The Princess of the Rushing Winds / The Lotus of the Palace of Air
swords.upright: Immaturity in the mental dimension, undefined self-identity, hesitation, confusion and indecision, difficulty making choices.
swords.narrative: Planning, analysis, discernment, new student of the mental dimension, discovering the importance of boundaries, learning about mental clarity.
swords.reversed: Mental confusion and scattered thinking, gossip or spreading misinformation, all talk and no action, defensiveness masking vulnerability.

swords.name: Knight of Swords
swords.title: The Prince of the Chariot of the Winds
swords.upright: Restlessness in the mental dimension, focusing on too many goals, confusion, hesitation, self-sabotage, failure to take responsibility for choices.
swords.narrative: Internalizing learning about the power of mental creation, analysis, and discernment, applying it actively in everyday life, remarkable alertness, clarity of thought.
swords.reversed: Recklessness, acting without thinking, verbal aggression, burnout from overextension, using intellect as a weapon rather than a tool.

swords.name: Queen of Swords
swords.title: The Queen of the Thrones of Air
swords.upright: Developed intellect, analytical and critical approach, anxiety from past experiences, paranoia, precise and quick judgment, good intentions.
swords.narrative: Awareness of serious responsibilities, blessed with fairness, justice, independence, multidimensional thinking, determination, decisiveness.
swords.reversed: Cold and cutting behavior, bitterness from past wounds, isolation disguised as independence, using sharp logic to avoid emotional truth.

swords.name: King of Swords
swords.title: The Lord of the Winds and Breezes / The King of the Spirits of Air
swords.upright: Maturity and full control over the mental process, taking responsibility for personal choices, optimism stemming from past lessons, careful and strategic actions.
swords.narrative: Mastery in the mental dimension, the power to change analytical thinking through motives of love, detachment from prejudices, forming advice based on universal wisdom.
swords.reversed: Abuse of authority, manipulation through intellect, cold-heartedness, making judgments without compassion, tyrannical or dishonest leadership.

cups.name: Page of Cups
cups.title: The Princess of the Waters / The Lotus of the Palace of the Floods
cups.upright: Emotional immaturity, inner world not understood, narcissistic personality, inability to penetrate deep feelings, vulnerability.
cups.narrative: Emotional reflection, understanding emotions that lead to creative imagination, new student of emotional life, heightened sensitivity, mystical and spiritual experiences.
cups.reversed: Emotional immaturity taken to an extreme, fantasy detached from reality, manipulation through vulnerability, inability to handle emotional depth.

cups.name: Knight of Cups
cups.title: The Prince of the Chariot of the Waters
cups.upright: Kindness, desire to help others, low self-awareness, tendency toward unfounded fantasies, slow and moderate progress due to lack of proper tools.
cups.narrative: Grace, calm, creative imagination, visionary thinking, internalized learning about the power of emotion and imagination applied in everyday life.
cups.reversed: Moodiness, emotional unavailability, romantic illusions collapsing, drifting without direction, using charm to avoid real commitment.

cups.name: Queen of Cups
cups.title: The Queen of the Thrones of the Waters
cups.upright: Developed inner dimension and fertile imagination, difficulty expressing emotions, emotional closure, defensiveness due to past painful experiences.
cups.narrative: Emotional and intuitive control, mediumship, imagination, creativity, emotional insight, prophetic vision, recognizing that what is seen in the mind's eye reflects the state of consciousness.
cups.reversed: Emotional manipulation, absorbing others' pain without boundaries, martyrdom, using intuition for self-serving ends, repressed feelings surfacing destructively.

cups.name: King of Cups
cups.title: The Lord of the Waves and the Waters / The King of the Hosts of the Sea
cups.upright: Maturity and full mastery over emotional processes, painful past does not affect optimism, excellent relational skills through investment and commitment.
cups.narrative: The ability to flow with life's highs and lows, expertise in inner observation, captain of one's self and domain, rich imagination full of creative possibilities in calm and turbulent times.
cups.reversed: Emotional suppression, volatility beneath a calm surface, using emotional intelligence to control others, avoidance of inner work.

wands.name: Page of Wands
wands.title: The Princess of the Shining Flame / The Rose of the Palace of Fire
wands.upright: Right aspirations, hesitation from lack of resources, dealing with too many matters, lack of continuity, need for guidance and a shift in approach.
wands.narrative: Thoughtful, aspiring to bring ideas and inspiration into the world, spiritual student, awareness of intentions and motives, openness to spiritual lessons.
wands.reversed: Scattered energy and lack of follow-through, creative blocks, false starts, impatience with the learning process, chasing inspiration without commitment.

wands.name: Knight of Wands
wands.title: The Prince of the Chariot of Fire
wands.upright: Correct alignment with personal desire, help from external sources, beginning of self-awareness, transition from narrow vision to seeing the bigger picture.
wands.narrative: Enthusiasm, focus, internalizing lessons and applying them in life, directing one's will with divine will in every situation.
wands.reversed: Impulsiveness without direction, ego-driven action, scattered focus, rushing ahead before foundations are solid, burning out quickly.

wands.name: Queen of Wands
wands.title: The Queen of the Thrones of Flame
wands.upright: Strong power and firm spiritual foundation, defense of what exists due to inner insecurity, passivity, excellent use of available resources, accumulation of power for future use.
wands.narrative: Control, sovereignty, connection with the divine, willpower, self-pride, respect, supernatural abilities, assertiveness, extroversion, self-confidence, guidance through love and understanding.
wands.reversed: Aggression born from insecurity, demanding attention, using personal power to intimidate, self-doubt hidden behind a dominant exterior.

wands.name: King of Wands
wands.title: The Lord of the Flame and Lightning / The King of the Spirits of Fire
wands.upright: Maturity and full mastery over the creative process, active and dynamic, courage to advance in a balanced way, balance between caution and initiative, broad vision.
wands.narrative: The ability for calm inner inspiration during intense times, courage, respect, control, superiority, the union of personal will with divine will, personal wholeness, self-actualization.
wands.reversed: Authoritarian or domineering behavior, impulsiveness in leadership, egotism, forcing creative vision onto others, loss of connection to higher purpose.
